We need to infer overall market sentiment based on data. The data includes Fed rates: high probability of no change (74% Yes) vs increase 25bps (25% Yes). So expectation: rates stay same -> bullish for equities? Usually no change is neutral to slightly bullish. Israel-Iran ceasefire continues through July 25: Yes 74% -> bullish for region stability. US invade Iran before 2027: Yes 30% -> low chance of conflict -> bullish. Mojtaba Khamenei head of state end 2026: Yes 72% -> expectation of continuity? Might be neutral. Mitch McConnell steps down: Yes 40% -> moderate chance. Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control: Yes 4% -> very low chance of loss of control -> bullish for oil supply stability. WTI crude hit $95 in July: Yes 65% -> expectation of higher oil prices -> could be bullish for energy, bearish for consumers.

Overall sentiment: seems bullish due to stable rates, ceasefire, low invasion odds, low Kharg loss, oil price up expectation. So Bullish.
